SIMD for Collision

In a previous post I discussed the big wins I got from graph coloring to make the contact solver faster. That same approach exists in Box3D.

In 3D there is an opportunity for using wide SIMD that does not exist in 2D. This benchmark drops 5120 convex hulls, each with 32 points.

HullVerticesFacesEdges
Box8612
Boulder325989

Box3D uses the Separating Axis Test for collision detection. Unfortunately, SAT in 3D has quadratic complexity.

for (Edge edgeA : hullA.edges) {
    for (Edge edgeB : hullB.edges) {
        TestCrossProduct(edgeA, edgeB);
    }
}

SSE2 is over twice as fast as scalar. These timings are for the entire simulation, not just the edge-edge test.

box3d

996 words

2026-07-17 17:00 -0700
